content:
Key Findings

  • West Lancashire Borough Council paid 20 suppliers over £1 million each between 2016/17 and 2025/26, totalling £162.6 million.
  • The largest single supplier was WATES PROPERTY SERVICES LTD, receiving £29.4 million.
  • Five suppliers received over £5 million each, accounting for £68.3 million of the total.
  • The council transacted with 4,614 unique suppliers during this period.
  • The top 20 suppliers represent just 0.43% of all suppliers but received 48.8% of the total spend.

The Million-Pound Club

West Lancashire Borough Council has paid 20 suppliers over £1 million each between 2016/17 and 2025/26. These top suppliers received a combined total of £162.6 million, representing nearly half (48.8%) of the council's total expenditure of £332.9 million over this period. This highlights the significant role that a small number of suppliers play in the council's spending.

The largest single supplier was WATES PROPERTY SERVICES LTD, which received £29.4 million. This amount is more than double the next highest supplier, TAWD VALLEY DEVELOPMENTS LTD, which received £21.0 million. The top five suppliers alone account for £68.3 million, or 20.5% of the total spend.

Top Suppliers and Their Roles

The top suppliers provide a range of services and goods to the council. WATES PROPERTY SERVICES LTD is likely involved in property management and maintenance, while TAWD VALLEY DEVELOPMENTS LTD may be involved in housing or infrastructure projects. LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL, which received £16.5 million, is likely providing shared services or infrastructure support.

Other notable suppliers include ST MODWEN with £8.1 million and RIVERSIDE TRUCK RENTAL LTD with £7.3 million. These suppliers likely provide essential services such as property development and vehicle rental, respectively. The presence of PWLB and PUBLIC WORKS LOAN BOARD with a combined £13.8 million indicates significant borrowing for capital projects.

Comparing the Top Suppliers

The following table lists the top 10 suppliers by total expenditure, highlighting the significant amounts paid to each.

Supplier Total Expenditure (£)
WATES PROPERTY SERVICES LTD £29.4M
TAWD VALLEY DEVELOPMENTS LTD £21.0M
LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL £16.5M
A CONNOLLY LTD £13.2M
PWLB £9.2M
ST MODWEN £8.1M
RIVERSIDE TRUCK RENTAL LTD £7.3M
RIBBLE VALLEY BOROUGH COUNCIL £6.6M
HEAT 2000 LTD £5.2M
ALLIANCE LEISURE SERVICES £4.9M

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to West Lancashire Borough Council about the specific services provided by WATES PROPERTY SERVICES LTD and TAWD VALLEY DEVELOPMENTS LTD.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to learn more about the council's spending priorities and procurement processes.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see the details of contracts awarded to the top suppliers.
  • Write to your local councillor about the significant amounts spent with PWLB and PUBLIC WORKS LOAN BOARD, and ask for more information on the council's borrowing and investment strategies.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse other aspects of West Lancashire Borough Council's expenditure.
This article is based on West Lancashire Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
